questions: You don't tend to feel grossed out or put off by things others find unpleasant. You open a container in the back of the fridge that's been there too long.
You don't tend to feel grossed out or put off by things others find unpleasant. A coworker describes a weird rash they found on their arm.
You don't tend to feel grossed out or put off by things others find unpleasant. You're cleaning the drain in your kitchen sink.
You don't tend to feel grossed out or put off by things others find unpleasant. Someone at lunch tells a story about finding a bug in their salad at another restaurant.
You don't tend to feel grossed out or put off by things others find unpleasant. You notice a stain on the table at a restaurant where you're about to sit.
You don't tend to feel grossed out or put off by things others find unpleasant. A friend describes a scene from a horror movie they watched.
You don't tend to feel grossed out or put off by things others find unpleasant. You walk past an overflowing public trash can on a warm day.
You don't tend to feel grossed out or put off by things others find unpleasant. Your dog rolls in something smelly at the park.
You don't tend to feel grossed out or put off by things others find unpleasant. Someone shares a video of an unusual-looking deep-sea fish.
You don't tend to feel grossed out or put off by things others find unpleasant. You're cleaning out the lint trap in the dryer and find a clump of hair mixed in.
